name: TcpConnect
title: TcpConnect
syntax: long TcpConnect( dword socket, dword address, dword port); // form 1
long TcpConnect( dword socket, byte ipv6Address[], dword port); // form 2
long TcpConnect( dword socket, IP_Endpoint remoteEndpoint ); // form 3
description: The function establishes a connection with the specified location.

Use IpGetLastSocketError to get a more specific error code. The specific error code is WSAWOULDBLOCK (10035), is no “real” error, but just confirms the non-blocking socket behavior. A real connection error is different from error 10035.
The CAPL callback OnTcpConnect will be called on completion (successful or not), provided it is implemented in the same CAPL program.
On the remote station OnTcpListen is called up, which accepts the connection with TcpAccept.

parameters.name: address
parameters.description: The numerical IPv4 address of the destination.

parameters.name: ipv6Address
parameters.description: The numerical IPv6 address of the destination in a 16 byte array.

parameters.name: port
parameters.description: The port of the destination in host-byte order.

parameters.name: remoteEndpoint
parameters.description: The destination endpoint. IP address and port number must be specified. Example: IP_Endpoint( 192.168.1.1:40001 ) IP_Endpoint( [FC00::0001]:40001 )

availability: CANoe Measurement Setup / CANalyzer Analysis Branch
CANoe Test Setup for Test Modules
